Subject: 🎮 Chaotic Suns — you're the first beta tester

Hi Sam,

I'm reaching out because I've built a game called Chaotic Suns — and you're the very first person I'm asking to play it.

It's a cosmic survival sim where you guide a civilization orbiting multiple unpredictable suns. The eras flip between Stable (safe) and Chaotic (everything burns). Your job: observe the orbital patterns, predict what comes next, launch space probes, research technologies, and try not to let everyone die.

I built it fast — about 4,700 lines of Python over the past few hours — and it needs real eyes on it. I'm way too close to the code to see what's confusing or broken.

WHAT YOU'D BE DOING:
• Play for 15-20 minutes (or as long as it holds your attention)
• Try the prediction mode (E key) — draw where you think a sun will go
• Open the Research Lab (T key) and unlock some techs
• Launch probes (keys 1-4) and see if they make sense
• Tell me what's confusing, broken, boring, or fun

HONEST QUESTIONS I NEED ANSWERED:
• Does the tutorial/help screen make sense or is it overwhelming?
• Can you figure out what to do without reading the help?
• Is the prediction mode intuitive or confusing?
• What made you want to quit?
• What made you want to keep playing?
• Any crashes or weird behavior?

THE GAME:
• Download: https://github.com/armstrongsam25/chaotic-suns
  (Grab the zip from dist/ChaoticSuns_0.2.0-beta_linux.zip)
• Requirements: Python 3.12+, pip install pygame numpy
• Runs on Linux/Mac/Windows

Quick start:
  python3 -m venv venv && source venv/bin/activate
  pip install -r requirements.txt
  python3 main.py

Controls cheat sheet:
  Space — Pause     E — Prediction Mode     H — Help
  T — Research Lab     1-4 — Launch Probes
  Arrows — Pan     +/- — Zoom     Esc — Menu

I'm not looking for a polished review — just raw feedback. What works, what doesn't, what made you go "wait, what?" Any bugs you hit, any UI that confused you, any mechanic that felt pointless.

This is literally the first cold beta test email. You're patient zero.

Reply here with whatever you've got.

— Sam
